title: Vending Machine With Quarters-Dimes- and Nickels
description: Suppose we have a vending machine that gives change. A customer selects an item for purchase and inserts a bill into the vending machine. The vending machine dispenses the purchased item and gives change. We will assume that all item prices are multiples of 5 cents, and the machine gives all change in quarters, dimes, and nickels. Construct a program that computes how many quarters, dimes, and nickels to return to the customer.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_dinamicka_alokacija_varijable_malloc
description: Napisati C program koji dinamički alocira memoriju za jednu cjelobrojnu varijablu. Program treba omogućiti korisniku da unese cijeli broj, smjesti ga u dinamički alociranu memoriju, ispiše unesenu vrijednost na ekran, te na kraju pravilno oslobodi zauzetu memoriju. Potrebno je provjeriti da li je alokacija memorije uspješno izvršena prije korištenja pokazivača.
author: Amina Hromic

title: c_funkcija_stepenuj
description: Napisati program koji koristi funkciju stepenuj za izračunavanje stepena cijelog broja. Ispisati vrijednosti stepenovanja brojeva 2 i -3 za stepene n = 0, 1, 2, 3 i 4.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Reporting the Total Hours Each Employee Worked
description:
Suppose we have an input file that contains information about how many hours each employee of a company has worked. The file looks like the following (Employee's name, Hours):
Erica 7.5 8.5 10.25 8 8.5
Erin 10.5 11.5 12 11 10.75
Simone 8 8 8
...
Construct a program that reads this file and calculates the total number of hours worked by each individual. Make sure that the program handles each specific exception that could occur.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Rotating the Array Values
description: Construct a program that has a method that receives an array of values and returns the array rotated to the left by 1 position so that the value at the front of the array goes to the back and the order of the other values stays the same. For example, if the array is {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}, the program will change it to {2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 1}.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Printing Sequence of Numbers with a Gap Between Adjacent Values
description: Construct a program that prints a sequence of numbers from 1 (inclusive) to 16 (exclusive) in increments of 4.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Processing a List of Temperature Values
description: Construct a program that reads a series of temperatures and reports the days that are above 32 degrees Fahrenheit. Assume that the temperature values are in Fahrenheit. Also assume that input values could have a decimal point.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: c_srednja_vrijednost_matrice
description: Napisati program koji ispisuje srednju vrijednost elemenata matrice.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Printing Sequence of Numbers with a Gap Between Adjacent Values
description: Construct a program that prints a sequence of numbers from 7 (inclusive) to 35 (inclusive) in increments of 7.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Receiving Input Integers Until a Certain Condition is Met
description: Construct a program that receives an integer from the user, outputs that integer, and stops receiving integers when the user enters a negative integer.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Greatest Common Factor
description: Construct a program that recursively finds and returns the great common factor between of two integers.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Calculating and Rounding Up Body Mass Index (BMI) To the Nearest Integer
description:
BMI is a measure of body fat based on height and weight that applies to adult men and women. BMI Categories are as follows: 
Underweight = <18.5
Normal weight = 18.5–24.9
Overweight = 25–29.9
Obesity = BMI of 30 or greater.
English BMI Formula (Imperial) is: BMI = (Weight in Pounds / (Height in inches x Height in inches)) x 703.
 BMI results are usually displayed as integers (values without decimal points). Construct a program to round the BMI result to the nearest integer.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Input Manipulation Challenge
description: Construct a program that takes a user's inputs and calculate the division of the inputs.Then convert the division result into an integer, check the current division result's type and print the original division result, converted result and the original division result's float absolute value.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: JSON ITERATION 1
description: Write the code that prints the all NFL stats for the superbowl winning teams from the year 2022 to 2025. You're provided with a simple json data assigned to a variable
author: Arun Balajiee Lekshmi Narayanan

title: Determining When a Student Fails a Course (Case 1)
description: Construct a program to determine whether a student fails the course based on the inputs that it receives from the instructor. The student fails the course if the exam score is less than 55 or student has more than 2 missing homework.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Insertion Sort
description: reads in Strings and uses insertion sort to print them out in sorted order
author: Summit Dang

description: C CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Pokazivaci na karaktere

description: Construct a program that prints the digits of an integer from right to left.
author: Roya Hosseini

description: Napisati program koji prima redni broj mjeseca sa tastature i ispisuje da li je on ispravan ili ne.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_ispis_unazad (while petlja)
description: Napisati program koji ispisuje na ekran brojeve od 1 do 5 unazad koristeći while petlju.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Printing the Squares of Numbers Within a Specified Range (Case 3)
description: Construct a program to write out the squares of every number between 20 and 25, both inclusive.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Encontrar el Divisor Mas Pequeno de un Numero Positivo
description: Construir un programa que encuentre el divisor más pequeño (distinto de 1) de un número positivo. Por ejemplo, el divisor más pequeño de 4 es 2.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Modifying an Array (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that increments all elements of the array by 1.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_zamjena_kolona_dva_mjesta_dalje
description: Napisati program koji pomjera svaku kolonu matrice na mjesto kolone koja se nalazi dva mjesta desno od nje. Brojanje kolona ide u krug: ako matrica ima 5 kolona, kolona s indeksom 0 dolazi na mjesto kolone 2, kolona 1 na kolonu 3, kolona 3 na kolonu 0 itd. Korisnik unosi elemente matrice dimenzija 4x5, a program ispisuje novu matricu nakon jednog prolaska zamjene svih kolona. Napomena: Preporučuje se koristiti privremenu kopiju matrice kako bi se izbjeglo prepisivanje vrijednosti dok se mijenjaju kolone.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Recursively calculate the sum of all numbers from 1 to n Challenge
description:
Using recursion, complete the sum1toN method so that it returns the total sum of all numbers from 1 to N.
Examples:


    sum1toN(3) => 6

    sum1toN(5) => 15
author: Rully Hendrawan

title: Three Booleans
description: Construct a program that determines whether at least one of the three boolean variables is true based on the inputs that it receives from the user.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Multiple Parameters Challenge
description: Construct a program that calculates the pay and current savings.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Calculating the Winning Percentage of a Sports Team (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that receives from the user the number of games in a tournament and the number of the games that a sports team won in the tournament, and calculates the winning percentage of that sports team. The program must ask the user for each of the two inputs until the user enters a valid number.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Warning the User about the Changes in the Temperature and Humidity
description: Construct a program that receives the temperature and humidity for today and yesterday and warns the user when it is getting colder or warmer or neither. When it is getting warmer, the program should also warn the user about the changes in humidity. In particular, it should warn the user when today's humidity is more, less, or has not changed compared to yesterday's humidity. Note that the temperature and humidity values could have a decimal point.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: The Class for Representing a Loan (Case 2)
description:
Construct a class that represents a loan. This class should contain data representing the interest rate, loan amount, and the length of loan period (in years). The class should have constructors that permits a loan to be created when:
 - annual interest rate, length of loan period, and loan amount are specified
 - none of the properties of the loan are specified. In this, we create a loan with default values for the loan properties (3 % interest rate, 1-year loan period, and 1000 loan amount).
The other methods of the this class should calculate the amount of monthly payments on the loan as well as the total payment.

The LoanTester2 class instantiates an object from this class and invokes its method to determine the amount of monthly payments on the loan and the total payment.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Pythagorean Theorem
description: Construct a program that accepts two input values from the user, one for each side of a right-angle triangle. The program uses the Pythagorean theorem (c^2 = a^2 + b^2) to calculate the length of the triangle's hypotenuse.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_kubiraj
description: Napisati funkciju kubiraj koja računa kub cijelog broja i u glavnom programu ispisati kubove brojeva od 1 do 5.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Passing Values
description: Construct a function that increases the original integer's value by 1 and return the increased value's memory address.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Mostrando los Dias que Estan por Sobre el Promedio de Temperatura
description: Construir un programa que lea una serie de temperaturas y reporte la temperatura promedio y el número de días que están por sobre el promedio. Suponga que los valores de entrada pueden tener un punto decimal.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: Determining the Average Ratings of each Respondent and Average Ratings Given to Each Soda in the Survey
description: Suppose a soda manufacturer held a taste test for four new flavors to determine if people liked them. The manufacturer got 10 people to try each new flavor and give it a score from 1 to 5, where 1 equals poor and 5 equals excellent. The results of that survey is stored in a matrix. Each row of the matrix holds the responses that all testers gave for one particular soda flavor, and each column holds the responses of one person for all sodas.\nConstruct a program that determines the average ratings that respondents provided to four new flavors of soda as well as the average ratings of each respondent.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Sequential Execution
description: Python CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Sequential Execution
author: Aditya Joshi

title: Printing the Squares of Numbers Within a Specified Range (Case 1)
description: Construct a program to write out the squares of even positive integers less than or equal to 10.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_selection_sort
description: Napisati program koji traži unos niza od 10 realnih brojeva sa tastature. Niz je zatim potrebno sortirati u rastućem redoslijedu pomoću Selection sort algoritma i ispisati sortirani niz na ekran.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Processing the Results of a Soda Survey (Case 2)
description: Suppose a soda manufacturer held a taste test for four new flavors to determine if people liked them. The manufacturer got 10 people to try each new flavor and give it a score from 1 to 5, where 1 equals poor and 5 equals excellent. The results of that survey is stored in a matrix. Each row of the matrix holds the responses that all testers gave for one particular soda flavor, and each column holds the responses of one person for all sodas.\nConstruct a program that determines the average ratings that respondents provided to four new flavors of soda.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Pythagorean Theorem (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that accepts two input values from the user, one for each side of a right-angle triangle. The program uses the Pythagorean theorem (c^2 = a^2 + b^2) to calculate the length of the triangle's hypotenuse.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: The Class for Representing a Bank Account (Case 2)
description:
Construct a class that represents a basic bank account. This class should contain data representing the name of the account’s owner, the account number, and the account’s current balance. The interest rate for the account is stored as a constant. The class should have constructors that permits an account to be opened when:
 - the account name, account number, and initial balance are specified
 - only the account name and the account number are specified. In this case, initial balance is assumed to be zero.
The other methods of this class should perform various services on the account (making deposits, withdrawals, adding interest to the account). These methods should examine the data passed into them to make sure the requested transaction is valid.

The Transactions2 class creates some bank accounts and requests various services.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Concatenando Caracteres de Dos Strings (Caso 1)
description: Construya un programa que tenga una función que reciba dos strings y devuelva un string formado a partir de los strings dados de tal manera que se omita el primer carácter de cada string.\nPor ejemplo, el nuevo string que se formará a partir de los strings 'sol' y 'luna' es 'oluna'.\nSuponga que ambos strings tienen al menos un carácter.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: Calculating the Average of the Input Integers that are an Even Number
description: Construct a program that reads a series of integer values from the user, sums up the integers that are an even number, and calculate their average. The user enters 0 to indicate the end of the input.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Warning about the Changes in the Weather Condition
description: Construct a program that receives the temperature for today and yesterday and warns the user when it is getting colder or warmer or neither. The temperature values could have a decimal point.

title: Pasar Valores

author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Vending Machine
description: Suppose we have a vending machine that gives change. A customer selects an item for purchase and inserts a bill into the vending machine. The vending machine dispenses the purchased item and gives change. We will assume that all item prices are multiples of 25 cents, and the machine gives all change in dollar and quarters. Construct a program that computes how many dollars and quarters to return to the customer.

title: Binary Search - 1
description: Construct a function that receives an array and a target element to be searched in the given array. If the element is present, return its index, otherwise return -1.

title: Finding Adjacent Duplicates in a Sequence of Numbers
description: Construct a program that checks whether a sequence of numbers, entered one at a time, contains adjacent duplicates. The user enters -1 to indicate the end of the input.\nFor example, 4 is a duplicate in the sequence of numbers 1, 3, 4, 4, -1.

description: In this example, we will learn how to create more functions inside a linked list

description: Construct a program that reverses the digits of an integer mathematically.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Determining When to Buy a New Phone (Case 2)
description:
Construct a program that determines whether it is time to buy a new phone based on the inputs that it receives from the user. A new phone should be bought in either of the following cases:
the phone breaks
the screen has a problem
the phone has the random shutdown problem
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Singly Linked List
description: Create the necessary classes for a Singly Linked List, containing the method append(item) to add a new item to the end of the linked list. Test the singly linked list after the classes are created.

author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Calculando el Porcentaje de Victorias de un Equipo (Caso 2)
description: Construya un programa que reciba del usuario el número de partidos en un torneo y el número de partidos que un equipo ganó en el torneo, y calcule el porcentaje de victorias de ese equipo. El programa debe pedir al usuario cada una de las dos entradas hasta que el usuario ingrese un número válido.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: Printing A Sequence of Repeated Numbers
description: Construct a program that receives an integer N from the user and prints a sequence of space-separated numbers from 1 to N such that the N-th number in the sequence is repeated N times.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Creating a List of Words from File(s)
description: Construct a program that reads a text file and creates a list of the words that the file contains, ignoring the case of the letters. Assume that all of the words in the text file are separated by a single space characters.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Equality Comparison Method
description: Construct a program with two classes, where each class has a comparison method. Test whether instances of each class is equal to each other using the comparison method.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Determining Whether One is a Teenager
description: Construct a program that receives a string that has the user name and age separated by a colon, and prints whether the user is a teenager. Make sure that the program handles all possible exceptions.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Reporting File Information
description: Construct a program that receives the full path to an input file from the user, reads that file and reports the number of lines, the longest line, and the number of words on each line. Make sure that the program handles each specific exception that could occur.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Calculating the Wage of an Employee at the Customer Service Call Center
description: Construct a program for the payment department of a company to calculate the wage of an employee who works at the customer service call center. Like other employees in the company, the employees at the customer service call center are paid based on the hours that they work. If they work over 40 hours in a week, the payment amount should take into account the overtime hours. The overtime hours are paid at a rate one and a half times the regular pay rate.\nThe company's policy is to pay more to those employees at the customer service call center who work during weekends. Therefore, the minimum extra pay that the employees could receive for each day of work during weekends is $30. The extra pay increases to the maximum of $50 for the employees who have at least 5 days of work during weekends.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Counting the Number of Valid and Banned Product Codes (Case 2)
description: Suppose a hypothetical company uses codes to represent its various products. A product code includes, among other information, a character in the tenth position that represents the zone from which that product was made, and a four-digit integer representing the district in which it will be sold. This four-digit integer begins at the 4th character and extends to the 7th character in the input code (inclusive). Due to some reorganization, products from zone R are banned from being sold in districts with a designation of 2000 or higher.\nConstruct a program that reads product codes from the user and counts the number of valid and banned codes entered. Make sure that the program handles all possible exceptions.\nThe program must ask the user for an input until the user enters STOP.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Printing the Total Number of Times Elements of One Array Appear in Another Array
description: Construct a program that has a method that receives two arrays and prints the total number of times the elements in the 2nd array appear in the 1st array. For example, if the 1st array is {1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 6} and the 2nd array is {3, 4, 5, 6, 7}, then the total number of times that the elements in the 2nd array appear in the 1st array is 6.\nNote that we need to count matches for all values in the 2nd array (including duplicate values, if any).
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Recursive Binary Search
description: Construct a function that uses recursive binary search to find if a specific value is in a list (known as a "key"). Recall: Binary search uses the "Divide and Conquer" strategy. Given a list of ordered integers, the key is searched by dividing the data list in half and only searching in the half that contains the key, and repeating the process until the key is found and the index of the key is returned. If the key is not found, -1 is returned.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

author: Roya Hosseini

title: Binary search Challenge
description:
Binary Search operates by dividing an array in half, and then determining whether or not the target value would be in the larger or smaller half. Then, this process repeats by dividing whichever half must contain the target in half again, over and over until the target is found.
Construct a function that performs binary search on a given array and returns the index of the target value if it is found, and -1 if it is not.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Determinando Si Uno Es Adolescente (Caso 1)
description: Construir un programa que reciba un string que tiene el nombre de usuario y la edad separados por dos puntos, y que imprima si el usuario es un adolescente. Asegúrate de que el programa maneje todas las posibles excepciones.

title: Maquina Expendedora Con Dolares y Monedas de Veinticinco
description: Supongamos que tenemos una máquina expendedora que da cambio. Un cliente selecciona un artículo para comprar e inserta un billete en la máquina expendedora. La máquina expendedora dispensa el artículo comprado y da cambio. Supondremos que todos los precios de los artículos son múltiplos de 25 centavos, y la máquina da todo el cambio en dólares y monedas de veinticinco. Construya un programa que calcule cuántos dólares y monedas de veinticinco devolver al cliente.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: c_pritisak
description: Napisati program koji deklariše cjelobrojnu varijablu pritisak i dodjeljuje joj neku vrijednost. Ako je vrijednost pritiska u intervalu [90, 120], ispisati na ekran "Pritisak je normalan". U suprotnom ispisati "Pritisak nije normalan".

title: The Class for Representing a Loan (Case 2)

 - annual interest rate, length of loan period, and loan amount are specified
 - none of the properties of the loan are specified. In this, we create a loan with default values for the loan properties (3 % interest rate, 1-year loan period, and 1000 loan amount).
The other methods of the this class should calculate the amount of monthly payments on the loan as well as the total payment.

The LoanTester2 class instantiates an object from this class and invokes its method to determine the amount of monthly payments on the loan and the total payment.

title: Printing Table of Medal Winner Counts with Row and Column Totals
description:
Assume that we have an array of countries that stores the names of the seven countries that participated in the Skating competitions at the Winter Olympic. We also have a 7x3 matrix that stores the number of medals that these seven countries won. This matrix looks like as follows:
{ {1, 0, 1 },
 { 1, 1, 0 },
 ...}
The first, second, and third numbers within a row i represent the number of Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals won by the country that is at the index i of the array of countries.
Construct a program that takes this matrix and the array of countries, and prints a table of medal counts with row and column totals. The column totals are the sum of the gold, silver, and bronze medals won in the competition. The output table should look like as follows:
      Country    Gold  Silver  Bronze   Total
         Canada        1       0       1          2
            China        1       1       0          2
           ...
Column Total      4       4       4

Program treba otvoriti datoteku u režimu za pisanje tako da se prilikom pokretanja kreira nova datoteka ili se postojeća datoteka briše. Nakon toga je potrebno omogućiti korisniku unos proizvoljnog broja knjiga sa tastature i upisati unesene podatke u datoteku koristeći funkciju fprintf, pri čemu se svaki zapis upisuje u poseban red u formatu: naziv autor godina. Overweight = 25–29.9
Obesity = BMI of 30 or greater.

description: U programu je deklarisan realan broj x i pokazivač double *p. Postavite p da pokazuje na x, promijenite vrijednost x preko p na 0.9 i ispišite x, p i vrijednost na koju pokazuje p.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_ubacivanje_rijeci
description: Napisati program koji u datom stringu zamjenjuje svaku cifru 1 sa tekstom "jedan". Pretpostaviti da je primljeni string dovoljno velik da primi sve dodatne karaktere.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Finding Adjacent Numbers in Ascending Order in a Sequence of Numbers
description: Construct a program that checks whether a sequence of non-zero numbers, entered one at a time, contains adjacent numbers in ascending order. Numbers are said to be in ascending order when they are arranged from the smallest to the largest number. For example, 5, 9, 13, 17 and 21 are arranged in ascending order.\nNote that you need to think what value should the program use to indicate the end of the input.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_pretvaranje_broja_u_slovo
description: Napisati program koji omogućava unos teksta sa maksimalno 1000 karaktera, a zatim u tekstu zamjenjuje sve brojeve sa slovom 'B'. Ispisati izmijenjeni string na kraju programa.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: The Class for Representing a Loan (Case 1)
description:
Construct a class that represents a loan. This class should contain data representing the interest rate, loan amount, and the length of loan period (in years). The class should have constructors that permits a loan to be created when:
 - annual interest rate, length of loan period, and loan amount are specified\n - none of the properties of the loan are specified. In this, we create a loan with default values for the loan properties (3 % interest rate, 1-year loan period, and 1000 loan amount).
The other method of the this class should calculate the amount of monthly payments on the loan.

The LoanTester1 class instantiates an object from this class and invokes its method to determine the amount of monthly payments on the loan.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: BMI Calculator
description:
BMI is a measure of body fat based on height and weight that applies to adult men and women. BMI Categories are as follows: 
Underweight = <18.5
Normal weight = 18.5–24.9
Overweight = 25–29.9
Obesity = BMI of 30 or greater.
English BMI Formula (Imperial) is: BMI = (Weight in Pounds / (Height in inches x Height in inches)) x 703.
 BMI results are usually displayed as integers (values without decimal points). Construct a program to round the BMI result to the nearest integer.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Updating an Element in a List (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that initializes a list with four string values, changes the last element in the list, and finally, prints the list.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

description: C CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Formatirani ispisi
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Aritmeticke operacije nad redovima i kolonama matrice

author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Informe de las Horas Totales Trabajadas por Cada Empleado (Caso 2)
description:
Supongamos que tenemos un archivo de entrada que contiene información sobre cuántas horas ha trabajado cada empleado de una empresa. El archivo se ve de la siguiente manera (Número de identificación del empleado, Nombre del empleado, Horas):
101 Erica 7.5 8.5 10.25 8 8.5
783 Erin 10.5 11.5 12 11 10.75
114 Simone 8 8 8
...
Construya un programa que lea este archivo y calcule el número total de horas trabajadas por cada individuo. Asegúrese de que el programa maneje cada excepción específica que podría ocurrir.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Point Class Hierarchy (Case 1)
description: Suppose we have a class Point and we want to extend it to produce class Point3D. The class Point has two data members, x and y that represent the point's integer coordinates in the two-dimensional space. A Point3D has three data members, x, y, and z that represent the point's integer coordinates in the three-dimensional space. Write the class declarations, the constructors, and the toString methods for these two classes.\n\nThe class InheritancePointTester1 tests these classes and their methods.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_upis_reda_na_kraj_datoteke
description: Data je datoteka knjige.txt koja u svakom redu sadrži sljedeće podatke razdvojene zarezom: cijena (realan broj), broj primjeraka (cijeli broj) i naziv (string). Napisati program koji dodaje novu knjigu u datoteku.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_vrijednosti_u_pokazivacima
description: Napisati program koji deklariše dvije varijable tipa long int i double, i dodjeljuje njihove adrese u odgovarajuće pokazivače. Zatim ispisati adrese tih varijabli i vrijednosti pohranjene u pokazivačima.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Hot-Dry Weather (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that determines whether it is too hot but not too dry based on the inputs that it receives from the user.

title: Searching Values of a List in Another List (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that has a function that receives two lists and prints the total number of times the elements in the 2nd list appear in the 1st list. For example, if the 1st list is [1, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5, 6] and the 2nd list is [3, 4, 5, 6, 7], then the total number of times that the elements in the 2nd list appear in the 1st list is 6.\nNote that we need to count matches for all values in the 2nd list (including duplicate values, if any).
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Tree traversal. Challenge
description: The tree can be traversed by deciding on a sequence to visit each node. There are three traversal algorithms: in-order traversal, pre-order traversal and post-order traversal. In-order traversal visits the nodes from the left, the root node and the right Pre-order traversal visits the nodes from the root node, the left and the right Post-order traversal visits the nodes from the left, the right and the root node. In this challenge, we construct an post-order traversal algorithm.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: c_unos_karaktera_getchar
description: Napisati program koji traži od korisnika da unese jedan karakter, koji se čita pomoću getchar() funkcije. Program treba zatim ispisati da li je znak veliko slovo, malo slovo ili nešto drugo.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

author: Roya Hosseini

title: The Wage of an Employee
description: Construct a program for the payment department of a company to calculate the wage of an employee who works at the customer service call center. Like other employees in the company, the employees at the customer service call center are paid based on the hours that they work. If they work over 40 hours in a week, the payment amount should take into account the overtime hours. The overtime hours are paid at a rate one and a half times the regular pay rate.\nThe company's policy is to pay more to those employees at the customer service call center who work during weekends. Therefore, the minimum extra pay that the employees could receive for each day of work during weekends is $30. The extra pay increases to the maximum of $50 for the employees who have at least 5 days of work during weekends.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Animal Class Hierarchy (Case 1)
description: Suppose we have a class Animal and we want to extend it to produce class Dog. An animal has a name. A Dog has a name and an owner. Write the class declarations, the constructors, and the toString methods for these two classes.\n\nThe class AnimalTester1 tests these classes and their methods.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Converting Milliseconds to Hours-Minutes- and Seconds
description: Construct a program that obtains hours, minutes, and seconds from an amount of time in milliseconds.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_zamjena_dva_reda
description: Napisati program koji zamjenjuje dva reda matrice (elementi jednog reda postaju elementi drugog reda, i obrnuto). Korisnik unosi indekse dva reda koja želi zamijeniti.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_strukture_funkcije_sredina_tacaka_s_pokazivacima
description: Napisati program koji definiše strukturu Tacka te fukciju "sredina" koja prima dvije varijable tipa ove strukture, računa središnju tačku te je vraća iz funkcije.

title: Desplazando los Valores de la Lista a la Izquierda Dos Posiciones
description: Construye un programa que tenga una función que reciba una lista de valores y devuelva la lista desplazada a la izquierda 2 posiciones, de modo que el valor al inicio de la lista vaya a la penúltima posición, el segundo valor de la lista vaya al final, y el orden de los otros valores se mantenga igual. Por ejemplo, si la lista es [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], el programa la cambiará a [3, 4, 5, 6, 1, 2].

title: c_dinamicka_alokacija_nizova_challenge
description: Napisati program koji koristi dinamičku alokaciju memorije koja je inicijalizirana s nulama za niz struktura. Definisati strukturu Student koja sadrži ime studenta i broj bodova. Program treba imati posebnu funkciju za alokaciju niza studenata i posebnu funkciju za oslobađanje memorije. Korisnik unosi broj studenata, zatim podatke za svakog studenta. Nakon unosa, program treba pronaći i ispisati podatke o studentu koji ima najveći broj bodova. Na kraju programa obavezno pravilno osloboditi dinamički alociranu memoriju.
author: Amina Hromic

title: Printing Consecutive Numbers Within a Specified Range
description: Construct a program that prints a sequence of numbers from 1 (inclusive) to 9 (inclusive).

author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Doubly Linked List Challenge
description: Create the necessary classes for a Doubly Linked List, containing the method insert_at_start(data) to add a new item to the beginning of the linked list, insert_at_end(data) to add a new item to the end of the linked list, delete_at_start() to delete the first item from the link, and an __str__ method to print out the doubly linked list. Test the doubly linked list after all classes and methods are created.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Contando el Numero de Codigos de Producto Validos y Prohibidos (Caso 2)
description: Supongamos que una empresa hipotética utiliza códigos para representar sus diversos productos. Un código de producto incluye, entre otra información, un carácter en la décima posición que representa la zona de la que se fabricó ese producto, y un número entero de cuatro dígitos que representa el distrito en el que se venderá. Este número entero de cuatro dígitos comienza en el cuarto carácter y se extiende hasta el séptimo carácter en el código de entrada (inclusive). Debido a una reorganización, los productos de la zona R están prohibidos para ser vendidos en distritos con una designación de 2000 o más.\nConstruya un programa que lea códigos de productos del usuario y cuente el número de códigos válidos y prohibidos ingresados. Asegúrese de que el programa maneje todas las posibles excepciones.\nEl programa debe pedir al usuario una entrada hasta que el usuario ingrese STOP.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Advertir al Usuario sobre los Cambios en la Temperatura y Humedad.
description: Construir un programa que reciba la temperatura y la humedad de hoy y de ayer y advierta al usuario cuando esté haciendo más frío o más calor o ninguno de los dos. Cuando esté haciendo más calor, el programa también debe advertir al usuario sobre los cambios en la humedad. En particular, debe advertir al usuario cuando la humedad de hoy es mayor, menor o no haya cambiado en comparación con la humedad de ayer. Tenga en cuenta que los valores de temperatura y humedad pueden tener una parte decimal.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: Concatenating Characters of Two Strings
description: Construct a program that has a function that receives two strings and returns a string formed from the given strings separated by a space character such that the first two characters of the given strings are swapped.\nFor example, the new string that will be formed from the strings 'abc' and 'xyz' is 'xyc abz'.\nAssume that both strings have at least two characters.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Modifying an Array
description: Construct a program that increments all elements of the array by 1.

title: Vending Machine With Quarters, Dimes, and Nickels
description: Suppose we have a vending machine that gives change. A customer selects an item for purchase and inserts a bill into the vending machine. The vending machine dispenses the purchased item and gives change. We will assume that all item prices are multiples of 5 cents, and the machine gives all change in quarters, dimes, and nickels. Construct a program that computes how many quarters, dimes, and nickels to return to the customer.

title: c_unos_niza_while

title: Informar Informacion del Archivo (Caso 2)
description: Construir un programa que reciba la ruta completa a un archivo de entrada del usuario, lea ese archivo e informe el número de líneas, la línea más larga, el número de palabras en cada línea y la longitud de la palabra más larga en cada línea. Asegúrate de que el programa maneje cada excepción específica que podría ocurrir. Además, el programa debe pedir al usuario una ruta de archivo válida hasta que el usuario ingrese una ruta de archivo que se pueda acceder sin ninguna excepción.

title: Converting the Letter Grade of a Student to Its Numeric Range
description:
Construct a program that receives a grade from the user and prints the numeric range for that grade using the following grading rules:
A for scores ≥ 90
B for scores ≥ 80
C for scores ≥ 70
D for scores ≥ 60
F for scores < 60
For example, if the user enters the grade D, the program prints "Score is in range [60-70)".

Funkcija prima dva cijela broja i pomoću pokazivača sprema u zadane varijable: manju vrijednost (min) i veću vrijednost (max). U glavnom programu deklarisati dvije cijele vrijednosti i varijable za rezultat, pozvati funkciju i ispisati dobijene vrijednosti.

title: c_brojanje_rijeci_alfanumericki

author: Roya Hosseini

title: Printing A Sequence of Repeated Numbers (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that receives an integer N from the user and prints a sequence of space-separated numbers from 1 to N such that each number in the sequence is repeated 5 times. For example, if N is 6, the program will print the following sequence:\n11111 22222 33333 44444 55555 66666
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Vending Machine With Quarters-Dimes- and Nickels
description: Suppose we have a vending machine that gives change. A customer selects an item for purchase and inserts a bill into the vending machine. The vending machine dispenses the purchased item and gives change. We will assume that all item prices are multiples of 5 cents, and the machine gives all change in quarters, dimes, and nickels. Construct a program that computes how many quarters, dimes, and nickels to return to the customer.

A Right triangle star pattern contains N asterisks in N-th row. Construct a program that receives the number of rows in the right triangle star pattern and prints that triangle. For example, when the number of rows in the right triangle star pattern is 5, the program prints the following output:
*
**
***
****
*****

description: Suppose a soda manufacturer held a taste test for four new flavors to determine if people liked them. The manufacturer got 10 people to try each new flavor and give it a score from 1 to 5, where 1 equals poor and 5 equals excellent. The results of that survey is stored in a matrix. Each row of the matrix holds the responses that all testers gave for one particular soda flavor, and each column holds the responses of one person for all sodas.\nConstruct a program that determines the maximum rating that each of the four new flavors of soda received.

description:
BMI is a measure of body fat based on height and weight that applies to adult men and women. BMI Categories are as follows: 
Underweight = <18.5
Normal weight = 18.5–24.9
Overweight = 25–29.9
Obesity = BMI of 30 or greater.
English BMI Formula (Imperial) is: BMI = (Weight in Pounds / (Height in inches x Height in inches)) x 703.
 BMI results are usually displayed as integers (values without decimal points). Construct a program to round the BMI result to the nearest integer.

title: Counting the Number of Valid and Banned Product Codes
description: Suppose a hypothetical company uses codes to represent its various products. A product code includes, among other information, a character in the tenth position that represents the zone from which that product was made, and a four-digit integer representing the district in which it will be sold. This four-digit integer begins at the 4th character and extends to the 7th character in the input code (inclusive). Due to some reorganization, products from zone R are banned from being sold in districts with a designation of 2000 or higher.\nConstruct a program that reads product codes from the user and counts the number of valid and banned codes entered. Make sure that the program handles all possible exceptions.\nThe program must ask the user for an input until the user enters STOP.

title: Determining When a Customer Could Rent a Car (Case 3)

Construye un programa que tenga una función que reciba un string del usuario y cree un diccionario que mapea cada carácter en el string dado a su frecuencia, es decir, cuántas veces aparece ese carácter en el string dado.
Por ejemplo, si el string dado es "libro", el programa crea el siguiente diccionario:
l : 1
i : 1
b : 1
r : 1
o : 1
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Reporting File Information (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that receives the full path to an input file from the user, reads that file and reports the number of lines, the longest line, and the number of words on each line. Make sure that the program handles each specific exception that could occur.

Construct a program that computes how many dollars and quarters to return to the customer.

Pre-order traversal visits the nodes from the root node, the left and the right

title: Printing Table of Medal Winner Counts with Row and Column Totals
description:
Assume that we have an array of countries that stores the names of the seven countries that participated in the Skating competitions at the Winter Olympic. We also have a 7x3 matrix that stores the number of medals that these seven countries won. This matrix looks like as follows:
{ {1, 0, 1 },
 { 1, 1, 0 },
 ...}
The first, second, and third numbers within a row i represent the number of Gold, Silver, and Bronze medals won by the country that is at the index i of the array of countries.
Construct a program that takes this matrix and the array of countries, and prints a table of medal counts with row and column totals. The column totals are the sum of the gold, silver, and bronze medals won in the competition. The output table should look like as follows:
      Country    Gold  Silver  Bronze   Total
         Canada        1       0       1          2
            China        1       1       0          2
           ...
Column Total      4       4       4

title: Imprimir Tabla de Conteo de Medallas con Totales por Fila y por Columna

[[ "Canada", 1, 0, 1 ],
 [ "China", 1, 1, 0 ],
 ...]
Cada fila de esta matriz corresponde a los conteos de medallas para el país en esa fila. El segundo, tercer y cuarto número dentro de una fila representan el número de medallas de Oro, Plata y Bronce ganadas por el país correspondiente en esa fila.
Construya un programa que tome esta matriz e imprima una tabla de conteos de medallas con totales de filas y columnas. Los totales de las columnas son la suma de las medallas de oro, plata y bronce ganadas en la competencia. La tabla de salida debería verse como sigue:
      Pais    Oro  Plata  Bronce   Total
         Canada        1       0       1          2
            China        1       1       0          2
           ...
Total de Columnas      4       4       4

title: Reporting the Total Hours Each Employee Worked (Case 2)
description:
Suppose we have an input file that contains information about how many hours each employee of a company has worked. The file looks like the following (Employee's identification number, Employee's name, Hours):
101 Erica 7.5 8.5 10.25 8 8.5
783 Erin 10.5 11.5 12 11 10.75
114 Simone 8 8 8
...
Construct a program that reads this file and calculates the total number of hours worked by each individual. Make sure that the program handles each specific exception that could occur.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_funkcija_djeljiv_sa
description: Napisati funkciju int djeljivSa(int x, int y) koja vraća 1 ako je x djeljiv sa y, a 0 ako nije. U glavnom programu tražiti unos dva broja od korisnika i ispisati da li je prvi uneseni broj djeljiv sa drugim.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Printing Digits of an Integer from Right to Left
description: Construct a program that prints the digits of an integer from right to left.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Nizovi karaktera
description: C CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Nizovi karaktera
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Determining the Weather Condition (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that determines whether it is either too hot or too dry (or both) based on the inputs that it receives from the user.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Mostrando los Dias que Estan por Sobre 32 Grados Fahrenheit
description: Construye un programa que lea una serie de temperaturas y reporte los días que están por sobre 32 grados Fahrenheit (0 grados Celsius). Supón que los valores de temperatura están en Fahrenheit. También supón que los valores de entrada podrían tener un punto decimal.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: c_promjena_vrijednosti_preko_pokazivaca_2
description: U programu su date cjelobrojne varijable a i b. Pokazivač p prvobitno pokazuje na varijablu a. Vaš zadatak je da promijenite vrijednost varijable b na 10 koristeći pokazivač p. Program ispisuje vrijednosti spremljene u a i b prije i poslije promjene.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: JSON Conditionals 2 Challenge
description: You're provide a JSON list of different books, find non-fiction books with rating greater than 4.5
author: Rully Hendrawan

title: Processing a List of Temperature Values
description: Construct a program that reads a series of temperatures and reports the average temperature and the number of the days that are above the average. Assume that the input values could have a decimal point.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Determining When a Student Fails a Course (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that determines whether a student fails the course based on the inputs that it receives from the instructor. The student fails the course if his/her score is not above the class average.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Printing the Squares of Numbers Within a Specified Range
description: Construct a program to write out the squares of even positive integers less than or equal to 10.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Printing an Inverted Right Triangle Star Pattern
description:
An inverted right triangle star pattern of N rows contains N-i+1 asterisks in the i-th row. Construct a program that receives the number of rows in the inverted right triangle star pattern and prints that triangle. For example, when the number of rows in the inverted right triangle star pattern is 5, the program prints the following output:
*****
****
***
**
*
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_brisanje_samoglasnika
description: Napisati program koji iz datog stringa briše sve samoglasnike.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: The Class for Representing a Bank Account
description: Construct a class that represents a basic bank account. This class should contain data representing the name of the account’s owner, the account number, and the account’s current balance. The interest rate for the account is stored as a constant. The constructor of the class should accept three parameters that are used to initialize the instance (i.e., object) data when an object is instantiated from this class. The other methods of this class should perform various services on the account (making deposits, withdrawals, adding interest to the account). These methods should examine the data passed into them to make sure the requested transaction is valid.\n\nThe Transactions1 class creates some bank accounts and requests various services.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Reporting File Information (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that receives the full path to an input file from the user, reads that file and reports the number of lines, the longest line, the number of words on each line, and the length of the longest word on each line. Make sure that the program handles each specific exception that could occur.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_dijeljenje
description: Napisati program koji na ekran ispisuje rezultat dijeljenja broja 5 sa 2, zaokružen na dva decimalna mjesta.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Time Converter
description: Construct a program that obtains minutes and remaining seconds from the input amount of time in seconds. For example, 500 seconds contains 8 minutes and 20 seconds.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_max_elementi_kolona
description: Napisati program koji za svaku kolonu ispisuje najveći element koji se nalazi u njoj.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_ispis_glavna_dijagonala
description: Napisati program koji od korisnika traži unos matrice cijelih brojeva dimenzija 3x3 i zatim ispisuje na ekran elemente na glavnoj dijagonali.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: La Clase para Representar un Prestamo (Caso 1)
description: Construya una clase que represente un préstamo. Esta clase debe contener datos que representen la tasa de interés, el monto del préstamo y la duración del período del préstamo (en años); todos los cuales solo se pueden acceder o cambiar a través de los métodos getter y setter. Una instancia de la clase debe ser creada especificando la tasa de interés anual, la duración del período del préstamo y el monto del préstamo.\nEl otro método de esta clase debe calcular el monto de los pagos mensuales del préstamo.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: PANDAS Statistics 1
description: From a class of 200 students, you have a dataset of students, their section, and grades. Start by inspecting the dataset. Get the sample size. Then, print top 5 examples. Next, get the mean. Also, try the pandas describe function. Get student counts by grade. Find students in section 2 and get the counts by grade.  Print the 10 entries in this result. Use the value_counts function
author: Rully Hendrawan

title: Renting a Car (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that determines whether a customer could rent a car based on the inputs that it receives from the car rental agent. Rental cars are available to licensed drivers of 21 years of age or over who have not had a car accident.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Comparing the List of Words from Two Files
description: Construct a program that reads two text files and creates a list of the words that each of these files contains, ignoring the case of the letters. The program should determine which list is larger in size, i.e., which list has more words.\nAssume that all of the words in these text files are separated by a single space characters.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_ispis_unazad (for petlja)
description: Napisati program koji ispisuje na ekran brojeve od 1 do 5 unazad koristeći for petlju.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Three Booleans (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that receives the value of three boolean variables from the user and determines whether all variables have the same value, either all three True or all three False.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: The Grade Letter
description:
Construct a program that receives a grade from the user and prints the numeric range for that grade using the following grading rules:
A for scores ≥ 90
B for scores ≥ 80
C for scores ≥ 70
D for scores ≥ 60
F for scores < 60
For example, if the user enters the grade D, the program prints "Score is in range [60-70)".
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Queue Class - Check if Element Exists
description: In this example, you will use Queue class from queue provided by python. This class contains methods for adding a new element to the end and retrieving the first element of the queue. However it does not contain a method to check if the queue contains an element. We will define a function that will traverse the queue and return True if the queue contains the given element.
author: Aditya Joshi

title: Time Converter
description: Construct a program that obtains hours, minutes, and seconds from an amount of time in milliseconds.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Creando un Diccionario de Pares Estudiante-Puntajes (Caso 2)
description: Supongamos que tenemos una lista de estudiantes y una lista de sus correspondientes puntajes de exámenes. Construya un programa que tenga dos funciones:\n- una función que recibe estas dos listas y devuelve un diccionario que mapea cada estudiante a las puntuaciones de ese estudiante, y \n- una función que recibe el diccionario creado por la función anterior y calcula el puntaje promedio de cada estudiante
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: c_citanje_izmjena_datoteka_koristenjem_kursora
description: Napisati program koji radi sa tekstualnom datotekom podaci.txt otvorenom u read-update režimu. Program treba prvo otvoriti datoteku i pomoću funkcije fseek pomjeriti kursor na kraj datoteke, a zatim pomoću funkcije ftell odrediti i ispisati trenutnu poziciju kursora (veličinu datoteke). Nakon toga program treba pomjeriti kursor na početak datoteke korištenjem fseek sa parametrom SEEK_SET i ispisati prvih nekoliko znakova iz datoteke. Na kraju, program treba ponovo ispisati trenutnu poziciju kursora koristeći ftell i zatvoriti datoteku. Ukoliko datoteku nije moguće otvoriti, program treba ispisati odgovarajuću poruku o grešci.
author: Amina Hromic

title: Printing Consecutive Numbers Within a Specified Range (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that prints a sequence of numbers from 8 (inclusive) to 14 (inclusive).
author: Roya Hosseini

title: La Clase para Representar un Prestamo (Caso 2)
description: Construya una clase que represente un préstamo. Esta clase debe contener datos que representen la tasa de interés, el monto del préstamo y la duración del período del préstamo (en años); todos los cuales solo se pueden acceder o cambiar a través de los métodos getter y setter. Una instancia de la clase debe ser creada especificando la tasa de interés anual, la duración del período del préstamo y el monto del préstamo.\nLos otros métodos de esta clase deben calcular el monto de los pagos mensuales del préstamo, así como el pago total.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: The Class for Representing a TV
description: Construct a class that represents a TV. The class should contain data that represents the TV's state (power on or off, current volume level). The state of the TV could be accessed or changed only through the getter and setter methods. The class should also include methods to change the state of the TV (turn on/off, change volume level). Assume that the volume level is an integer and ranges from 1 to 7 (both inclusive).
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Creating an Array of Numbers/Strings
description: Construct a program that creates an array of first ten positive odd numbers.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Updating an Element in the Array (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that initializes an array with three integers, changes the first element in the array, and finally, prints all the array elements.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_strukture_i_nizovi
description: Napisati program u programskom jeziku C koji omogućava rad sa studentima koristeći strukture i nizove. Potrebno je definisati strukturu Student koja sadrži ime i prezime studenta, broj indeksa, niz od pet ocjena i prosjek ocjena. Program treba omogućiti unos podataka za tri studenta, pri čemu se za svakog studenta unose lični podaci i ocjene. Nakon unosa, potrebno je izračunati prosjek ocjena za svakog studenta korištenjem posebne funkcije. Zatim je potrebno sortirati niz studenata po prosjeku ocjena u rastućem redoslijedu koristeći selection sort algoritam i na kraju ispisati podatke o studentima nakon sortiranja.
author: Amina Hromic

title: c_max_array
description: Napisati program koji traži unos niza 10 cijelih brojeva sa tastature i zatim ispisuje najveći element u nizu.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Determining When a Student Fails a Course (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that determines whether a student fails the course based on the inputs that it receives from the instructor. The student fails the course if his/her score is not above the class average.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Processing Soda Survery (Case 3)
description: Suppose a soda manufacturer held a taste test for four new flavors to determine if people liked them. The manufacturer got 10 people to try each new flavor and give it a score from 1 to 5, where 1 equals poor and 5 equals excellent. The results of that survey is stored in a matrix. Each row of the matrix holds the responses that all testers gave for one particular soda flavor, and each column holds the responses of one person for all sodas.\nConstruct a program that determines the average ratings that respondents provided to four new flavors of soda as well as the average ratings of each respondent.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: String Comparison (Case 2)
description: Construct the program that modifies the string "hexxo worxd!" so that it matches (ignoring case) the string "HELLO WORLD!".
author: Roya Hosseini

title: String With Escape Characters (Case 2)
description:
Construct a program that prints the following output:
""Two Double Quotes""
        Slashes //
How '"confounding' "" it is!
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Binary search
description:
Binary Search operates by dividing an array in half, and then determining whether or not the target value would be in the larger or smaller half. Then, this process repeats by dividing whichever half must contain the target in half again, over and over until the target is found.
Construct a function that performs binary search on a given array and returns the index of the target value if it is found in the array. Assume that the target will be in the array (in other words, do not handle the case where the target does not exist in the array).
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Informacion de Herencia de Persona y Empleado
description: Construir dos clases: una que represente a una Persona y otra que represente a un Empleado que debe ser definida como una subclase de Persona. Las clases deben definir funciones para mostrar la información del empleado.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Calculo del Indice de Masa Corporal (IMC)
description:
El IMC es una medida de la grasa corporal basada en la altura y el peso que se aplica a hombres y mujeres adultos. Las categorías de IMC son las siguientes:
Bajo peso = <18.5
Peso normal = 18.5–24.9
Sobrepeso = 25–29.9
Obesidad = IMC de 30 o más.
La fórmula del IMC es: IMC = (Peso en kilogramos / (Altura en pulgadas x Altura en pulgadas)).
Construya un programa que calcule el Índice de Masa Corporal (IMC) de acuerdo con esta fórmula.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: JSON ITERATION 1
description: Write the code that prints the all NFL stats for the superbowl winning teams from the year 2022 to 2025. You're provided with a simple json data assigned to a variable
author: Rully Hendrawan

title: Insertion Sort
description: Java CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Insertion Sort
author: Summit Dang

title: The Class for Representing a TV (Case 2)
description: Construct a class that represents a TV. The class should contain data that represents the TV's state (power on or off, current volume level). The class should include methods to access the state of the TV and also methods to change the state of the TV (turn on/off, change volume level). Assume that the volume level is an integer and ranges from 1 to 7 (both inclusive). \n\nThe class TVTester2 instantiates two objects from this class and invokes the methods on those objects to turn on the TV and change the volume level.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Determining When a Customer Could Rent a Car (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that determines whether a customer could rent a car based on the inputs that it receives from the car rental agent. Rental cars are available to licensed drivers of 21 years of age or over who have not had a car accident.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Updating Two-Dimensional Array (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that initializes a 2x4 two-dimensional matrix that has multiples of 10 from 10 to 80 for entries, sets the second element of the first row to be 8, and finally prints all the elements in the matrix.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Processing the Digits of an Integer
description: Construct a program that prints the digits of an integer from right to left.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_pokazivaci_manipulacija_clanova_niza
description: Dat je niz cijelih brojeva i pokazivač p koji pokazuje na drugi član niza. Uvećati vrijednost drugog člana niza za 1 preko pokazivača p i ispisati je. Zatim izračunati i postaviti novu vrijednost pretposljednjeg elementa niza tako što se njegova prethodna vrijednost pomnoži sa 2, te i tu novu vrijednost ispisati.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_unos_i_ispis_datuma
description: Napisati kod koji kreira strukturu datum te funkcije za unos i ispis datuma.
author: Amina Hromic

title: c_formatirane_datoteke_citanje_broja_i_stringa
description: Neka svaki red u datoteci datoteka.txt sadrži jedan broj i jedan string, razdvojeni razmakom. Napisati program koji će ispisati na ekran sve stringove jedan ispod drugog i sumu svih brojeva koji se nalaze u datoteci.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Updating Two-Dimensional List (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that initializes a 3x3 two-dimensional matrix that has the numbers 1 through 9 for entries, updates the middle row to a list filled with 1s, then sets the top-right element of the matrix to be 10, and finally prints the matrix.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: c_izbacivanje_nte_rijeci
description: Napisati program koji iz datog stringa izbacuje n-tu riječ po redu. Brojanje počinje od 1 (prva riječ se treba obrisati ako je n = 1). Riječ je niz karaktera omeđena razmacima.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Solving Josephus Problem Challenge
description: Construct a program by using Queue structure to solve a Josephus problem. Josephus problem is a counting-out game. N people are in a circle, labeled as 1, 2, 3, … ,n. Given a skip number k, counting from the person labeled as 1, in every round, after k number of people are skipped, the next person will be counted out of the circle. Repeat the process with the remaining people until only one person remains. Then free the last one person. The program should return the last freed  person.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Printing Consecutive Numbers Within a Specified Range
description: Construct a program that prints a sequence of numbers from 1 (inclusive) to 9 (inclusive).
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Reporting the Total Hours Each Employee Worked (Case 1)
description:
Suppose we have an input file that contains information about how many hours each employee of a company has worked. The file looks like the following (Employee's name, Hours):
Erica 7.5 8.5 10.25 8 8.5
Erin 10.5 11.5 12 11 10.75
Simone 8 8 8
...
Construct a program that reads this file and calculates the total number of hours worked by each individual. Make sure that the program handles each specific exception that could occur.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_funkcija_podstring
description: Napisati funkciju podstring koja prima dva stringa s1 i s2, te provjerava da li je s2 podstring stringa s1. Ako jeste, funkcija treba vratiti pokazivač na prvi znak u stringu s1 koji predstavlja početak podstringa, u suprotnom treba vratiti nul-pokazivač.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Accessing String Characters (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that prints the 7th, 11th, and 14th character of the string "Second Example!".
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Calculating the Average of the Input Integers that are an Even Number
description: Construct a program that reads a series of integer values from the user, sums up the integers that are an even number, and calculate their average. The user enters 0 to indicate the end of the input.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Maquina Expendedora Con Monedas de diferentes centavos
description: Supongamos que tenemos una máquina expendedora que da cambio. Un cliente selecciona un artículo para comprar e inserta un billete en la máquina expendedora. La máquina expendedora dispensa el artículo comprado y da cambio. Supondremos que todos los precios de los artículos son múltiplos de 5 centavos, y la máquina da todo el cambio en monedas de 25, 10 y 5 centavos. Construya un programa que calcule cuántas monedas de 25, 10, y 5 centavos debe devolver al cliente.
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Recursively calculate the power of a number
description:
Using recursion, complete the power method so that it returns the value of a to the power of b.
Examples:

    power(3,4) => 81

    power(2,8) => 256

    power(1,1) => 1

    power(9,0) => 1
author: Rully Hendrawan

description: Construct a program to write out the squares of odd positive integers less than 10.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

description: Write a program that finds the minimum value in an array.

description: Construct a program that determines whether it is too hot but not too dry based on the inputs that it receives from the user.

Asegúrate de que el programa maneje todas las posibles excepciones. El programa debe pedir al usuario una entrada hasta que el usuario ingrese un string que pueda ser procesada sin ninguna excepción.

title: Calculating the Winning Percentage of a Sports Team (Case 3)
description: Construct a program that receives from the user the number of games that a sports team won and tied in a tournament of 12 games and calculates the winning percentage of the sports team, counting ties as half wins. The program must ask the user for each of the two inputs until the user enters a valid number.

author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_datoteke_brojanje_rijeci_s_velikim_pocetnim_slovom
description: Napisati program koji određuje broj riječi koje počinju velikim slovom u tekstualnoj datoteci. Ime datoteke se unosi sa tastature. Riječ je definisana kao niz karaktera omeđen razmacima, tabulatorima ili novim redom (whitespace karakterima).
author: Mubina Kamberovic

description: C CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Unos i ispis struktura

author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: JLinkedListUtil Challenge
description: This LinkedListUtil class tests various usages of the LinkedList class. The single param is  an array of string. You will create a linked list with the string elements and return the  linked list with all but the 1st two elements removed. (source: codecheck)
author: Rully Hendrawan

title: Printing the outline of a square star pattern Challenge
description:
Construct a program that takes an input as the size of a square and print the outline of the square.
For example, if a user’s input is 4, the printed square should look like:
****
*  *
*  *
****
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: c_dinamicka_alokacija_niza_calloc
description: Napisati program koji koristi dinamičku alokaciju memorije za cjelobrojni niz. Program treba imati posebnu funkciju za alokaciju niza i posebnu funkciju za oslobađanje zauzete memorije. Korisnik unosi broj elemenata niza, zatim unosi vrijednosti elemenata. Nakon unosa, program treba ispisati elemente niza u obrnutom redoslijedu. Na kraju programa obavezno pravilno osloboditi dinamički alociranu memoriju.

title: c_celzijus_farenhajt_konverzija
description: Napisati program koji traži unos broja stepeni u Celzijusima i pretvara ih u Farenhajte prema formuli: F = C * (9/5) + 32. Program treba ispisati na ekran obje vrijednosti sa jednim decimalnim mjestom.

****
****
****
****

description: Construct a program that calculates the average of all the values in an array. The program should handle arrays with arbitrary number of values (including an empty array).
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Rotating the List Values to the Right by Two Position
description: Construct a program that has a function that receives a list of values and returns the list rotated to the right by 2 position so that the value that is currently at the end of the list is moved to the second position, the second last value of the list is moved to the front of the list, and the remaining values are shifted to the right. For example, if the list is {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}, the program will change it to {5, 6, 1, 2, 3, 4}.

title: Determining the Weather Condition (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that determines whether it is either too hot or too dry (or both) based on the inputs that it receives from the user.

When it is getting warmer, the program should also warn the user about the changes in humidity. In particular, it should warn the user when today's humidity is more, less, or has not changed compared to yesterday's humidity. title: Printing A Right Triangle Star Pattern

An inverted right triangle star pattern of N rows contains N-i+1 asterisks in the i-th row. title: Imprimir Tabla de Conteo de Medallas con Totales por Fila
description:
Supongamos que tenemos una matriz de 7x4 que almacena el número de medallas que siete países ganaron en las competencias de patinaje en los Juegos Olímpicos de Invierno. Esta matriz se ve como sigue:
[[ "Canada", 1, 0, 1 ],
 [ "China", 1, 1, 0 ],
 ...]
Cada fila de esta matriz corresponde a los conteos de medallas para el país en esa fila. Los segundo, tercero y cuarto números dentro de una fila representan el número de medallas de Oro, Plata y Bronce ganadas por el país correspondiente en esa fila.
Construya un programa que tome esta matriz e imprima una tabla de conteos de medallas con total por fila que muestre el número de medallas de Oro, Plata, Bronce y Total para cada uno de los países que participaron en la competencia. La tabla de salida debería verse como sigue:
    Pais    Oro  Plata  Bronce   Total

       ...

description: Napisati program koji čita podatke iz tekstualne datoteke rezultati.txt. Datoteka sadrži više redova, pri čemu je svaki red zapisan u sljedećem formatu: prezime studenta, ime studenta i broj bodova, razdvojeni razmacima. Program treba učitati sve zapise iz datoteke i na osnovu učitanih podataka ispisati ukupan broj studenata, broj studenata koji su položili ispit (studenti sa 40 ili više bodova), te podatke o studentu koji ima najveći broj bodova (prezime, ime i broj bodova). Ukoliko datoteka ne postoji ili je prazna, program treba ispisati odgovarajuću poruku o grešci.
author: Amina Hromic

title: Doubly Linked List
description: Create the necessary classes for a Doubly Linked List, containing the method insert_at_start(data) to add a new item to the beginning of the linked list, insert_at_end(data) to add a new item to the end of the linked list, delete_at_start() to delete the first item from the link, and an __str__ method to print out the doubly linked list. Test the doubly linked list after all classes and methods are created.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Desplazando los Valores de la Lista a la Izquierda Una Posicion
description: Construye un programa que tenga una función que reciba una lista de valores y devuelva la lista desplazada a la izquierda por 1 posición, de modo que el valor al inicio de la lista vaya al final y el orden de los otros valores se mantenga igual. Por ejemplo, si la lista es [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6], el programa la cambiará a [2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 1].
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: Calculating the Winning Percentage of a Sports Team (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that receives from the user the number of games in a tournament and the number of the games that a sports team won in the tournament, and calculates the winning percentage of that sports team. The program must ask the user for each of the two inputs until the user enters a valid number.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: Increment/Decrement Operators (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that performs increment/decrement operations on variable a (with initial value of 4) and variable b (with initial value of 5) and prints the following output as the result of these operations:\n-1\n3
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: The Class for Representing a Point in the Euclidean Plane (Case 2)
description: Construct a class that represents a point in the Euclidean plane. The class should contain data that represents the point’s integer coordinates (x,y). The class should also include getter and setter methods for accessing and changing the point's coordinates and a method to calculate and return the point's distance from the origin (0,0).\n\nThe class PointTester2 instantiates an object from this class, sets the (x,y) coordinates of the point, and calculates the distance between this point and origin.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Binary Search - Recursive
description: Python CodeConstruction&CompletionBundle for demonstrating Binary Search - Recursive
author: Aditya Joshi

title: Determining When a Student Fails a Course (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that determines whether a student fails the course based on the inputs that it receives from the instructor. The student fails the course if the exam score is less than 55 or when the student has cheated.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Input Manipulation
description: Construct a program that takes a user's inputs and multiplies the inputs.Then print the type of the result, convert the result to float and print its float absolute value.
author: Kamil Akhuseyinoglu

title: Calculo y Redondeo del Indice de Masa Corporal (IMC) al Entero Mas Cercano.
description:
El IMC es una medida de grasa corporal basada en la altura y el peso que se aplica a hombres y mujeres adultos. Las categorías de IMC son las siguientes: 
Bajo peso = <18.5
Peso normal = 18.5–24.9
Sobrepeso = 25–29.9
Obesidad = IMC de 30 o más.
La fórmula del IMC es: IMC = (Peso en kilogramos / (Altura en metros x Altura en metros)).
Los resultados del IMC generalmente se muestran como enteros (valores sin puntos decimales). Construya un programa para redondear el resultado del IMC al entero más cercano.
author: Jordan Barria-Pineda

title: Modifying an Array (Case 2)
description: Construct a program that swaps pairs of adjacent elements of the array. For example, if the array is {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}, the program will change it to {2, 1, 4, 3, 6, 5}.\nThis program assumes that the array has always an even number of elements.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: Determinando Cuando Comprar un Nuevo Telefono (Caso 2)
description:
Construir un programa que determine si es el momento de comprar un nuevo teléfono basado en las entradas que recibe del usuario. Se debe comprar un nuevo teléfono en cualquiera de los siguientes casos:
el teléfono se rompe
la pantalla tiene un problema
el teléfono tiene el problema de apagado aleatorio
author: Isaac Alpizar-Chacon

title: The Sign of a Number
description: Construct a program that determines whether an integer is positive, negative, or zero.
author: Roya Hosseini

title: c_datoteke_brojanje_redova_rijeci_znakova
description: Napisati program koji određuje broj redova, broj riječi i broj znakova (pri čemu se "whitespace" znakovi ne računaju) u tekstualnoj datoteci čije se ime unosi sa tastature.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: Determining When a Customer Could Rent a Car (Case 1)
description: Construct a program that determines whether a customer could rent a car based on the inputs that it receives from the car rental agent. Rental cars are available to licensed drivers of 21 years of age or over.
author: Mohammad Hassany

title: c_funkcija_prebroji_znak
description: Implementirati funkciju prebroji koja prima string i karakter, te vraća broj pojavljivanja tog karaktera u stringu. U glavnom programu omogućiti unos stringa i ispis broja pronađenih pojavljivanja.
author: Mubina Kamberovic

title: c_pokazivaci_pomjeranje_kroz_niz
description: Dat je niz cijelih brojeva i pokazivač p koji pokazuje na prvi član niza. Program treba ispisati vrijednost prvog i drugog člana niza koristeći samo pokazivač p (pokazivačku aritmetiku). Zatim pomjeriti pokazivač da pokazuje na posljednji član i ispisati taj član, a nakon toga vratiti p za jedno mjesto unazad i ispisati pretposljednji član niza.
author: Mubina Kamberovic
